source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Subsea Leak at MC 778 Thunderhorse South

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Subsea Pipeline Leak
actions_taken: ROV inspection initiated on 6 June 2022 to investigate reported sheen
Two wells (MC 822-11/TD002 and MC 822-12/TD003) shut in around 1220 hours on 6 June 2022
P2 side of loop treated with Low Dosage Hydrate Inhibitor (LDHI)
Dead oil circulation procedure completed on 9 June 2022
Connector Actuation Tool (CAT) installed on 13 June 2022 to reseal gasket
Pressure testing completed with no further release observed
Routine maintenance and pigging approved for P2 flowline during intermittent operations
event.summary: On 8 June 2022 at 1310 hours, production personnel at MC 778 Thunderhorse South reported an external subsea leak at the P2 Riser Touchdown Connector - Pipeline End Termination (PLET) No. 5. The leak was discovered during a ROV survey initiated following a platform blackout on 5 June 2022. An estimated 12.8 to 128.1 gallons of crude oil was released. The root cause was determined to be connector failure on the PLET, potentially due to design flaw, improper installation, or manufacturing defects. The leak was temporarily sealed using a Connector Actuation Tool pending permanent replacement.
recommendations: Gulf of Mexico Regional Office Production Operation Support Section to review subsea leak detection system effectiveness
Conduct root cause analysis of connector leak once removed from service
Implement routine maintenance and pigging of Thunder Horse South Flowlines every 8-12 weeks to prevent buildup
Maintain P2 flowline out of service when not circulating or pigging until permanent replacement is completed

threats.name: Connector gasket failure due to wear and tear
Design flaw or manufacturing defect in connector

consequences.name: Subsea oil release to marine environment
Environmental pollution - sheen observed in satellite imagery

controls.name: C-ROC Subsea Leak Detection System
controls.lod_basis: Sensor-based algorithmic detection system monitoring pressure trends

supporting_text: BSEE investigated BP's subsea leak detection system to determine whether it was effective in identifying this leak. BP's subsea leak detection system known as C-ROC uses a conditional rate of change (which is an algorithmic sensor-based leak detection technique) which detects changes in pressure trends. BSEE confirmed that the system was active at the time of the leak but could not detect this relatively small discharge.

controls.name: ROV Subsea Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Visual detection and identification of subsea leaks
controls.lod_basis: Remote operated vehicle survey following platform blackout and reported sheen

supporting_text: On 6 June 2022, a subsea inspection of the DC45 Thunderhorse South field was being performed by a Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) because of a reported sheen of unknown origin passing near the field. The ROV found an external subsea leak at the P2 Riser Touchdown Connector at PLET #5

controls.name: Well Shut-in Procedure
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedure to stop flow through leaking connector

supporting_text: After the treatment, the two wells, MC 822-11/TD002 (S44) and MC 822-12/TD003 (S45), flowing up P2 riser were shut in (around 1220 hours).

controls.name: Connector Actuation Tool (CAT) Installation
controls.barrier_role: Temporary resealing of failed connector gasket
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical tool to apply pressure and reseal gasket to mating hub

supporting_text: On 13 June 2022, a Connector Actuation Tool (CAT) was installed onto the P2 Riser Touchdown connector. The CAT applied pressure to reseal the gasket to the mating hub. BP then pressure tested the flowline and did not observe any further release.

controls.name: Dead Oil Circulation Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Removal of hydrocarbon inventory from leaking segment
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedure to displace hydrocarbons with inert fluid

supporting_text: On 9 June 2022, a dead oil circulation procedure, begun on 8 June, was successfully completed with a few observations of a bubble during circulation.

controls.name: Routine Flowline Maintenance and Pigging
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of buildup and blockage in flowlines
controls.lod_basis: Scheduled maintenance every 8-12 weeks to prevent tar and asphaltene accumulation

supporting_text: BP shall also conduct routine maintenance of the Thunder Horse South Flowlines (including the P2 flowline via pigging). This is part of regular Thunder Horse South Loop maintenance (conducted about every 8-12 weeks) to prevent build up/blockage within the flowlines due to tar, asphaltenes, etc.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
